queue: Audit whether all CI guard workflows that protect 028 are PR-only and path-filtered away from their own workflow files.
Audit mode-registry structural invariants outside `advisorRouting` (`agent`, `packet`, `command`, `artifactRoot`) against the tests that claim registry parity.
Audit every doctor YAML for flag definitions that appear in allowed_flags but have zero handling logic in the workflow phases (orphan flags)
Trace whether --no-snapshot is actually wired into the checkpoint/snapshot logic in /doctor:update's YAML or if it's a dead flag across the entire doctor surface
Audit positional angle-bracket placeholders (e.g., <spec-folder>, <topic>) that are passed as positional arguments to MCP tools but don't match required schema parameter names.

Check whether workflow YAMLs manually remap the mismatched flags; if so, the drift is between command hint and YAML input schema rather than MCP tool schema, and the linter should validate against YAML schemas too.
Cross-check the speckit presentation contracts and any command tests for the same allowed-tools drift, since complete.md already contradicts its YAMLs.
Audit only 028 benchmark/roadmap/changelog tables that survived the cleanup for `*_V1` names and classify each as canonical-now, legacy-with-mapping, or deleted-with-deprecated-row.
Extend the parity check to deep-research and ai-council agents, which are also deep-thinking roles and may have the same model/reasoning asymmetry and missing Codex-mismatch rationale.
Audit `model_reasoning_effort = "high"` in every `.codex/agents/*.toml` against cli-codex SKILL.md default `medium`; the same stale files are doubly mismatched.
Check whether `.codex/config.toml:3` default `sandbox_mode = "danger-full-access"` contradicts cli-codex SKILL.md's explicit, safe-by-default sandbox discipline.
Audit generated Codex agent TOMLs for stale model pinning alongside effort pinning
Cross-check `.opencode/agents/*.md` permission blocks and `.codex/agents/*.md` tool grants for the same code-graph/detect_changes body-reference gaps
Audit OpenCode agent allowed-tool frontmatter for code-index tool names that depend on missing or mismatched MCP server grants.
Audit all create/assets/* code_search_note entries against their matching command allowed-tools lines for missing mcp__mk_code_index__code_graph_query.
Audit description.json files in the 028 tree for parallel source-path drift; they are generated by the same context pipeline and may carry the same bare lib/ convention

Inspect the graph-metadata generation code (.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/scripts/dist/memory/generate-context.js and mcp_server/lib/graph/graph-metadata-parser.ts) to see if it writes bare lib/ paths when invoked from the mcp_server directory
Expand the audit to other packet trees (outside 028) for the same bare lib/ convention in graph-metadata.json; 31 of 82 mcp_server-referencing 028 graph-metadata files are affected
Check graph-metadata.json for the same 001-007 phase-parent misclassification pattern, especially whether last_active_child_id and lean-trio expectations are populated consistently.
Audit child spec.md importance_tier and description.json metadata for bulk-copied parent values that were not tuned per phase
Cross-check implementation-summary.md scaffold boilerplate against actual completion status; identical 'Status PLANNED...' paragraphs are pasted across many phases and may mask completed work
Audit runtime tests for manually constructed `ExecutorConfig` objects that bypass `parseExecutorConfig` invariants.
Check 03x-04x graph-metadata for non-script repo-misrelative code paths such as mcp_server/lib/... and lib/search/... that should be rooted under .opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/.
Audit cli-opencode graph-metadata.json and skill-advisor trigger phrases to confirm opencode-go keywords no longer route users to sk-prompt-models after the gateway removal.
Determine whether fallback-router.ts is actively invoked by any runtime path that loads model_profiles.json, or if it is currently dead code masked by the schema mismatch.

Extend the retired-glob sweep to the /doctor manual testing playbooks under .opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/manual_testing_playbook/23--doctor-commands/ and 24--local-llm-query-intelligence/, which contain shell examples using context-index__*.sqlite for checksums, ls, find, and test commands.

Check whether doctor_update.yaml coverage scripts (lines 398-447) and mcp-doctor.sh correctly resolve canonical context-index.sqlite versus per-profile vector shards when probing vector health and coverage.
Audit deep workflow assets for `mcp_tool: memory_save` / `memory_index_scan` references whose command frontmatter lacks those MCP tools.
Audit doctor command allowed-tools for destructive/repair tools admitted without matching bounded read diagnostics such as `memory_list`, `memory_match_triggers`, or `memory_quick_search`.

Cross-check every command .md allowed-tools against the canonical TOOL_DEFINITIONS in .opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/tool-schemas.ts to find allowed-but-unregistered or registered-but-unallowed tools.
Audit executable offline parity gates, not just docs, for hard-coded `expectedCount: 37` or `expected = {"spec-memory": 37}` values that can keep stale manual docs passing.
Audit all /doctor read-only routes with mcp_tools: [] against the shared router frontmatter to identify script-only routes that still inherit mutating MCP capabilities.

Audit every 028 command YAML `mcp_integration.tool` / `allowed-tools` frontmatter to see whether memory_index_scan_status/cancel are systematically excluded, revealing the de facto 37-tool subset the stale docs assume.

Trace the tool count from 42 (post-embedder commit eb9563fba3) down to 37 (pre-async-scan commit e5f694a05e) via git history to identify the five removed tools and document why the embedder trio survived while others did not.
all: Command<->impl drift: each command doc (.opencode/commands/**/*.md) vs its asset YAML/script/handler — flags, routes, documented behavior vs actual.
Skill-doc<->code drift: SKILL.md claims (versions, flags, tool surfaces, counts) vs scripts/dist actual behavior.
Undocumented features: code paths, env vars (ENV_REFERENCE.md), MCP tools, CLI flags present in code but absent from any doc.
Documented-but-removed: doc/changelog claims for features no longer in code (dead refs, legacy names, opencode-go remnants).
Agent drift: .claude/.opencode/.codex agent defs — cross-runtime agreement + match to the skills/commands that dispatch them (tools, model, routing).
Changelog<->reality: 028 changelog tracks (incl 007-dark-flag-graduation, v3.7.0.0) vs shipped code — versions, dates, claims.
Reference/asset consistency: references/*.md and templates vs the real schema/DB layout/contracts (e.g. embedder + vector-shard docs vs actual DB).
MCP tool-surface drift: documented tool counts (39/8/9) and schemas vs registered tools + the daemon-CLI fallbacks.
Spec metadata/graph integrity: description.json/graph-metadata.json across 028 children; phase-parent invariants; coverage gaps.
Naming/routing consistency: gate-3 classifier, advisor maps, mode-registry.json vs actual packets; deprecated aliases (/ultrareview) and renames (ai-council->deep-ai-council).

CLI flag to MCP property-name mapping audit across memory commands — verify every bracketed flag in argument-hints maps to a real tool schema property (e.g. `--older-than`/`--folder` vs `olderThanDays`/`specFolder`).
Route manifest mutation-class consistency sweep — check every `_routes.yaml` target for allowed_flags that imply mutating operations versus declared `mutating:` class and authorized `mcp_tools`.
Audit command coverage matrices and allowed-tools frontmatter against current `TOOL_DEFINITIONS` across memory/speckit/doctor routers.
Extend ENV_REFERENCE drift checks beyond `SPECKIT_*` tokens to non-prefixed operator envs used by 028 search paths.

Version numbering drift: ENV_REFERENCE.md uses multiple version-label columns ('graduated', 'current', 'spec 017', 'PI-A2', etc.) but lacks any mapping to the v3.x.x.x release tags. Cross-check whether the flags claimed as 'graduated' in v3.7.0.0 changelog actually have their defaults flipped in search-flags.ts.
Codex model/reasoning-effort assignment audit — all 13 agents run gpt-5.4 with high effort; check if role-appropriate (e.g., deep-review may benefit from a different model than code)
MCP tool resolution audit — verify every skill/command that dispatches agents passes MCP tool names matching each runtime's convention

Tool-name convention drift: .claude uses mcp__mk_code_index__detect_changes (review) vs mcp__mk_code_index__code_graph_query (deep-context) — audit whether these granular forms are deliberate or should be unified

Sweep remaining workflow YAMLs and test fixtures for other hardcoded legacy provider/model strings dropped in cli-opencode v1.3.15.0 (e.g., `glm-5.1`, `qwen3.6-plus`, `kimi-k2.6`, `opencode-go/kimi-k2.6`).
Scan graph-metadata.json key_files/source_docs paths across all 028 children for other dead or repo-misrelative entries.
Audit migrated:true flags and heavy-doc presence at every 028 phase parent to identify further lean-trio violations.

Cross-check every command/agent `allowed-tools` list against the actual `TOOL_DEFINITIONS` and `advisor-tool-schemas` registries to find omitted or stale tool IDs (e.g., `skill_graph_propagate_enhances`, `embedder_status`, `session_health`).

Run the offline smoke check and `node .opencode/bin/spec-memory.cjs list-tools` against a built dist to confirm whether the spec-memory CLI currently reports 37 or 39 tools, and identify which two tools are filtered or counted differently.
Shard-split reference pass for `context-index__`, `resolveActiveProfileDbPath`, `vec_<dim>`, and `active_vec` wording across memory references.
Provider-cascade consistency pass across `environment_variables.md`, `embedder_architecture.md`, `embedding_resilience.md`, and `ENV_REFERENCE.md`.

Authorized MCP tool capability vs. route mutating class — audit every read-only route's mcp_tools for tools whose schemas expose mutating parameters (memory_index_scan, memory_causal_stats backfill, code_graph_apply, advisor_rebuild, skill_graph_scan)

Verify whether the drift-guard test (routing-registry-drift-guard.vitest.ts) is actually passing in CI or if it has been disabled/skipped — the prior finding at line 281 suggests misalignment that would cause a test failure
Add an automated linter that extracts bracketed `--flags` from command `argument-hint` frontmatter and runs them through `resolvePropertyName()` against the targeted MCP tool schemas.
Run the same flag/property lint on `/doctor` route `allowed_flags`, especially memory and causal-graph targets.

Cross-check whether the CLI daemon front doors (.opencode/bin/spec-memory.cjs, code-index.cjs, skill-advisor.cjs) pass these undocumented envs to the daemon child, or whether CHILD_ENV_ALLOWLIST blocks them silently
Check whether MEMORY_BASE_PATH/MEMORY_DB_PATH precedence over SPECKIT_DB_DIR is tested or documented anywhere beyond inline code comments
Audit doctor_update.yaml phase_5 dependency.order execute tool invocations against update.md allowed-tools for unreachable tools
Audit all speckit YAML mcp_integration.tool and mcp_tool keys systematically against their router frontmatter
Audit all v3.7 changelog claims that say a behavior is on for everyone against the actual flag helper default in `search-flags.ts`.
Audit packet 028 docs for `_V1` env names after the 009 flag-name cleanup and verify each has an ENV_REFERENCE legacy mapping.

Claude vs OpenCode MCP tool permission model divergence — .claude uses tools: frontmatter while .opencode uses permission: block + mcpServers: list; audit whether there is a canonical mapping document or if both surfaces are maintained independently and drift silently
Determine whether the cli-codex gpt-5.5 lock was introduced after `.codex/agents/*.toml` were authored, leaving the agent packaging stale.
Cost/latency audit: quantify the waste of running all 13 agents at high effort when several roles (context, markdown, review) map to medium per the cli-codex selection strategy.

Cross-runtime parity check: compare `.claude/agents/deep-review.md` and `.opencode/agents/deep-review.md` against `.codex/agents/deep-review.toml` to see if the non-Codex runtimes explicitly avoid the deep-thinking-on-Codex mismatch.

Cross-check each `.claude/agents/*.md` `tools:` line against body references for missing code-graph/detect_changes grants.
Verify OpenCode command `allowed-tools:` normalization: `mcp__mk_code_index__*` vs native `mk-code-index_*` tool names.
